project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Persistent Device Identifier (appInstallID UUID) + User Behavior Telemetry
project_privacy_data_flows.description: Cross-cutting: Wikipedia sends a persistent device installation UUID (appInstallID) with EVERY API request via the X-WMF-UUID HTTP header. Additionally, the Event Platform collects detailed user interaction telemetry (page views, edit attempts, sessions, errors) with the same identifier. This is not a feature-specific flow but affects all API interactions.

flow_steps.code: val appInstallID: String get() { var id = Prefs.appInstallId; if (id == null) { id = UUID.randomUUID().toString(); Prefs.appInstallId = id }; return id }
flow_steps.details: Random UUID generated on first app launch and persisted in SharedPreferences indefinitely. Used as a persistent device installation identifier.
X-WMF-UUID header containing the persistent appInstallID is attached to EVERY outbound HTTP request to Wikipedia APIs. This enables cross-session user tracking without explicit login.
Analytics events queued in an output buffer for batch transmission.
Batched events sent to analytics event intake endpoint with 30-second debounce.

flow_steps.code: abstract class MobileAppsEvent(streamName: String) : EventWithDt(streamName) { abstract val event: MobileAppsEventObj }
flow_steps.details: Every analytics event includes: app_install_id (persistent UUID), app_session_id (random session hex), is_anon (boolean), is_temp (temporary account flag).

flow_steps.code: private fun submitEditAttemptEvent(action: String, editorInterface: String, pageTitle: PageTitle) { EventPlatformClient.submit(EditAttemptStepEvent(EditAttemptStepInteractionEvent(action, WikipediaApp.instance.appInstallID, ..., pageTitle.prefixedText, ...))) }
flow_steps.details: Edit lifecycle events (init, saveIntent, saveAttempt, saveSuccess, saveFailure) include: appInstallID, editor interface type, device type, user ID, page title, namespace, wiki name.
Client-side errors reported with stack traces, HTTP context (URL, status code, method), and appInstallID.
Sampling/stream configuration fetched from server to control which analytics events are submitted.
